question_text: A puddle of water disappears on a sunny day through evaporation. If the water molecules take an average of 15 minutes to escape from the puddle, and the puddle loses 5 cm³ of water every 3 minutes, how much water (in cm³) will have evaporated after 45 minutes?
explanation: The puddle loses 5 cm³ every 3 minutes. In 45 minutes, there are 45 ÷ 3 = 15 intervals of 3 minutes. So the total evaporation is 5 × 15 = 75 cm³. (Note: the average time of 15 minutes is extra information and not needed for this calculation.)
